todos.content: Read MAS and extract all RTL requirements for cortex_m0_core
todos.activeForm: Reading MAS and extract all RTL requirements for cortex_m0_core
todos.detail: Read full MAS (1785 lines) from cortex_m0_core/mas/cortex_m0_core_spec.md. Extract all RTL requirements from §2-§8: module hierarchy, ports, parameters, FSM states, ALU ops, decoder, register file, NVIC, SysTick, exception handling, memory, timing, reset, coding style.

Write §1 Overview in <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md.
Todo:
- [ ] One-paragraph functional description: what the module does, why it exists
- [ ] Key design goals (latency, throughput, area, power targets if specified)
- [ ] List of key features (bullet form)
- [ ] Intended use context (SoC subsystem, standalone IP, etc.)
Gate: §1 complete before moving to §2.

Write §2 in <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md.
Todo:
- [ ] Instantiation tree (who instantiates this module, what sub-modules it contains)
- [ ] Port table: every signal with Name / Width / Direction / Clock Domain / Description
- [ ] Parameters table: Name / Default / Description for every parameter
- [ ] Confirm all port names and widths against requirement file (if present)
Gate: All ports explicitly defined before §3.

Write §3 in <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md.
Todo:
- [ ] For each major feature/mode: Trigger / Datapath / Control / Output
- [ ] FSM state table: State / Next State / Condition / Output Actions — every row
- [ ] No undefined state: every state transition condition must be explicit
- [ ] Note any pipelined stages or back-pressure signals
Gate: FSM has no ambiguous transitions.

Write §4 in <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md.
Todo:
- [ ] Register table: Offset / Name / Width / Access (RW/RO/WO/W1C) / Reset / Description — every register
- [ ] Bitfield detail for every non-trivial register: Bits / Name / Access / Description
- [ ] Reserved bits marked explicitly (RSVD, write 0, read undefined)
- [ ] Offsets match requirement file if specified; flag any conflict
Gate: Every CSR offset is unique and fully described.

Write §5 in <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md.
Todo:
- [ ] Interrupt source table: Source / Bit / Type (level/edge) / Enable Reg / Status Reg / Description
- [ ] irq output polarity and type (level/pulse, active-high/low)
- [ ] Clear mechanism: W1C on INT_STATUS, or auto-clear, or software-clear
- [ ] Masking logic: which enable bits gate which sources to irq
- [ ] If no interrupts: write '§5 N/A — no interrupt outputs'
Gate: Every interrupt source has a unique bit and clear path.

Write §6 in <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md.
Todo:
- [ ] Memory instance table: Instance / Type (SRAM/FIFO/RF) / Depth / Width / Read ports / Write ports / Latency / Description
- [ ] Read/write latency in clock cycles for each instance
- [ ] Initialization state (unknown / zero / don't-care at reset)
- [ ] Any ECC or parity requirements
- [ ] If no internal memory: write '§6 N/A — no internal memory'
Gate: All memory instances have defined latency and port count.

Write §7 in <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md.
Todo:
- [ ] Clock frequency (MHz) and period (ns)
- [ ] Input-to-output pipeline latency (cycles)
- [ ] Throughput (transactions per cycle at full load)
- [ ] Critical path description (longest combinational path)
- [ ] CDC crossings: list every clock domain boundary, synchronizer type used
- [ ] Setup/hold requirements for external interfaces (if any)
Gate: Clock frequency and latency are numeric, not vague.

Write §8 in <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md.
Todo:
- [ ] Coding style rules: nonblocking (<=) in always_ff, blocking (=) in always_comb
- [ ] Reset: synchronous or asynchronous, polarity (active-low rst_n preferred)
- [ ] Latch prevention: every always_comb branch must assign all outputs
- [ ] Lint waivers: list any expected lint warnings and justify each
- [ ] Pipeline constraints: any required register stage insertions
- [ ] Tie-off rules for unused inputs
Gate: §8 complete and consistent with §2 port list.

Write §9 in <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md.
Todo:
- [ ] Test Sequence table: ID / Sequence Name / Steps / Expected Result / Priority — minimum S1-S6
  - S1: Power-on reset
  - S2: Basic operation (configure → enable → poll done)
  - S3: Interrupt flow (enable → trigger → W1C clear)
  - S4: Memory R/W (write pattern → read back → compare)
  - S5: Back-to-back ops
  - S6: Error injection
- [ ] Functional coverage goals: FSM states, interrupt sources, register bits, memory boundary conditions
- [ ] SVA assertions list: at minimum — no illegal FSM transitions, irq deassert after W1C
- [ ] Known corner cases / hazards
Gate: All 9 sections complete. Save final MAS as <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md.
